text: Produce and publish a sub-3-minute Build Week demo
description: Public YouTube video under 3 minutes, in English or with English translation. Show the working project, the new hackathon extension, and explain how Codex and GPT-5.6 were used. Suggested arc: problem (intent disappears into issue trackers/chat) -> .bowman graph beside code -> dependency/provenance demo -> why this matters for open-source teams. Avoid third-party music/trademarks without permission.

reflections.context: The public demo remains institutionally human-dependent, but all preparatory work can be completed autonomously.
reflections.outcome: Created a timed 2:35 English demo script, exact screen/voice sequence, and privacy/capture checklist in meta/applications/openai-build-week-2026/demo-script.md.
reflections.effectiveness: The script stays focused on the judge-visible intent → context → Codex → Git evidence loop and explicitly separates human decisions from GPT-5.6/Codex work.
reflections.lesson: Keep the recorded walkthrough narrower than the overall product; the provenance proof is understandable within three minutes.

reflections.context: Felix confirmed he will create the required public video, leaving subtitle and narration packaging as autonomous support work.
reflections.outcome: Added a 214-word narration-only teleprompter and six-cue SRT subtitle file synchronized from 00:00 to 02:35. Updated the submission checklist.
reflections.effectiveness: The human recording step now needs only screen capture and reading; captions can be imported directly rather than recreated in the editor.
reflections.lesson: For institutionally human deliverables, reduce the handoff to the smallest possible mechanical action and preserve exact timing.

status.comment: Timed 2:35 script and capture checklist are complete. Felix must record the running UI, upload publicly to YouTube, and return the URL.
